Fetched robots.txt for www.uregina.ca, ctl.uregina.ca, and library.uregina.ca. Selected paths were not disallowed. Library robots included a general crawl-delay and specific disallowed query/script paths, not the selected guide pages.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a formal University of Regina GenAI operations policy, an official Provost academic-integrity message to students, CTL faculty/instructor GenAI guidance, and Archer Library student-use and citation guidance. A student blog-style edusites page and an event listing were rejected as unsuitable policy evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
Generative AI at the U of Regina

stopConditions: Use only official University of Regina controlled sources.
Do not infer student permission to use AI beyond program, course, assignment, or instructor-specific directions.
Do not treat event pages, student blogs, or external tool lists as policy evidence.

Academic Integrity and Artificial Intelligence
Generative AI at the U of Regina

artifacts.claimText: University of Regina policy OPS-080-050 says all University use of generative AI must be ethical, reliable, transparent, secure, and compliant with applicable laws and regulations; the policy primarily addresses governance and operations uses by employees.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Formal GenAI operations policy for employee governance and operations use.

citation.citationTitle: Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Regina policy treats data used to develop, operate, share, manage, or record GenAI systems as institutional data that must align with the University's Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy policy and data handling standards.
artifacts.normalizedValue: GenAI project and system data is institutional data governed by privacy and data handling rules.

citation.citationTitle: Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For GenAI projects or systems, University of Regina employees are directed to identify, assess, and analyze risks including privacy and security, and University-wide GenAI projects, tools, systems, or intended enterprise-data uses require approval through established IT project approval processes.
artifacts.normalizedValue: GenAI projects require risk assessment, and university-wide or enterprise-data GenAI uses require IT project approval.

citation.citationTitle: Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Regina Provost's message to students says individual instructors may determine whether and to what extent AI is permitted in course work, and warns that improper or unauthorized AI use can constitute academic misconduct.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Student AI use is course/instructor-specific, and unauthorized use can be academic misconduct.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Regina CTL guidance for faculty and instructors recommends that instructors set clear GenAI rules and syllabus statements, require students to disclose AI use when incorporated into coursework, and protect student privacy and intellectual property in teaching uses of GenAI.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Faculty/instructor guidance recommends clear rules, disclosure, and privacy/IP protection for GenAI in coursework.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI at the U of Regina
citation.publisher: University of Regina Centre for Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Regina CTL guidance says Turnitin is the only institutionally adopted plagiarism- and AI-detection tool, the AI-detection report is not conclusive proof of academic misconduct, and instructors are not permitted to use non-approved tools for AI detection.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Turnitin is the institutionally adopted AI-detection tool; non-approved AI-detection tools are not permitted.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI at the U of Regina
citation.publisher: University of Regina Centre for Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Archer Library's student-facing GenAI guidance tells students to check whether they have permission to use AI in their program or coursework, follow assignment instructions, verify GenAI output accuracy, and cite GenAI in their work.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Library guidance frames acceptable GenAI use around permission, assignment instructions, accuracy checks, and citation.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Archer Library's GenAI citation guide says AI-generated materials should generally be treated as a non-recoverable source and/or akin to personal communication, while students should check with their instructor if in doubt about when to cite AI and which style to use.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Library citation guidance advises instructor confirmation and style-specific treatment for AI-generated materials.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Narrowly scoped to the official operations policy and its employee governance/operations focus.
Supported by direct data-handling language in the formal policy.
Combines adjacent risk-assessment and approval sentences from the same Processes section without expanding beyond university-wide or enterprise-data GenAI uses.
Scoped to the official Provost message to students; does not infer a universal AI ban or permission.
Uses recommendation language from CTL guidance rather than treating the page as a formal policy.
Supported by explicit CTL language about Turnitin and non-approved AI-detection tools.
Student-facing library guide is treated as guidance, not as a binding university-wide academic-integrity policy.
Citation claim is scoped to Archer Library guidance and instructs students to confirm with instructors when in doubt.

artifacts.title: University of Regina AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official University of Regina sources include a formal Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence operations policy, a Provost message on academic integrity and AI, CTL faculty/instructor guidelines, and Archer Library student-use and citation guidance. Claims are staged as agent-reviewed candidates and are not canonical.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The CTL and library sources are guidance pages; only OPS-080-050 is treated as a formal policy source.
Student AI permission is instructor/course/assignment-specific based on the official sources reviewed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
